title: Sub-lemma 2a: y_i takes value 0 or >= min_j w_j; only negative z_i value is -1/n

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Context.** Set $z_i=y_i-\tfrac1n$ where $y_i=\langle w,r_i\rangle$. The proof of Claim 2 needs that $-1/n$ is the only distinct negative value among the $z_i$ (so the MMS hypothesis / its weakened variant applies cleanly).

**Obligations.**
1. Show $y_i=\sum_{j:\,M_{ij}=1} w_j$, which is $0$ if no column's single $1$ lies in row $i$, and otherwise a sum of one or more weights, hence $y_i\ge \min_{j}w_j>0$. Thus $y_i\in\{0\}\cup[\min_j w_j,\infty)$.
2. Note $\min_j w_j>0$ is a **fixed** constant depending only on $w,m$. Since $\tfrac1n=\tfrac1{n_0 t}\to0$ as $t\to\infty$, for all sufficiently large $t$ we have $\tfrac1n<\min_j w_j$. Conclude the only negative value taken by $z_i$ is $-\tfrac1n$ (occurring exactly when $y_i=0$).
3. **Faithfulness check:** make explicit that this "single negative value" observation is only needed for the closing *remark* (that a weakened MMS, with all negative entries equal, suffices); the main proof invokes the **full** MMS conjecture, which allows arbitrary real $z_i$. The main argument must not be made to depend on the unproven weakened variant.
